perm_expansion.note: Each -p char expands to a whole syscall group. r=21 syscalls, w=26 syscalls (both >20). A 'one rule = one syscall' model understates breadth ~20x+ per watch.

rules.note: single-file READ watch on /etc/passwd; -p r expands to 21 read syscalls (permtab.h). Reads are the highest-volume/lowest-signal class (f3 §7); breadth comes from the 21-syscall expansion, not from recursion (single file).
single-file WRITE/modify watch on /etc/passwd; -p w expands to 26 write syscalls (permtab.h). Writes to /etc/passwd are far rarer than reads, so a lower band than the read watch.

rate_band_basis: f3 §6 broad-watch anchor (low1k/typ20k/high200k) for the read watch; the write watch is ~0.1x (writes to a single identity file are rare). Both are single-file (not recursive); the breadth signal is the permtab syscall-group expansion.
